appliedUpdates.description: Updated known-issues.md: marked empty artistImageUrl issue as RESOLVED (fixed in commit 9a75986). Added documentation for testing Next.js Image components — do not assert on src attribute, use alt text instead.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance: When a feature description says 'throughout the app' or 'ALL files', workers must use grep/rg to audit the entire codebase for the pattern, not just the files they're already modifying. Example: 'Replace ALL raw tags' means running 'rg "
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature real-product-images-and-seed worker left 8 raw tags because they only replaced tags in files they were already modifying, despite the feature description saying 'Replace ALL raw tags... throughout the app'. Required a fix feature (fix-img-tags-and-seed) to address.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether standard Tailwind palette colors (e.g., orange-50, red-500) are acceptable alongside custom design tokens, or whether all colors must map to semantic tokens. Currently AGENTS.md says 'ALWAYS use semantic token classes' but sport-specific accent colors (basketball orange, football green) may not have semantic equivalents.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature sports-banners-and-event-images used default Tailwind orange/red colors in BasketballCard (from-orange-50, to-red-50, from-orange-500, to-red-500) because no semantic token existed for basketball-specific theming. These aren't arbitrary hex values but aren't custom tokens either.

rejectedObservations.observation: The pattern for replacing with Next.js using fill+object-cover is now consistently applied across the entire codebase.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in AGENTS.md under Images section: 'ALWAYS use Next.js Image component', 'Use fill with object-cover for responsive images in containers'.

rejectedObservations.observation: The ui-worker skill's Step 5 verification commands are adequate and skill procedure was followed correctly.
rejectedObservations.reason: Not actionable — confirms existing skill works as intended. No changes needed.
